description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the i386/rust:1 image for i686 jobs and the amd64/rust:1 image for x86_64 and pages jobs, defines a global MSRV variable, runs a before_script that executes getconf LONG_BIT, rustup self update, rustup --version, copies any existing cargo/registry/cache into $CARGO_HOME, removes $CARGO_HOME/registry/src, and lists cached crates, and an after_script that cleans up orphaned crates, removes the local cargo directory, recreates cargo/registry, and copies back the registry cache. Add an i686-gnulinux job (default test stage) that caches the cargo/ directory, runs only on the ci pipeline, and runs rustup toolchain install --profile minimal beta-$TARGET $MSRV-$TARGET nightly-$TARGET followed by cargo +beta-$TARGET test with fail‑on‑warnings arbitrary borsh num‑traits serde std, cargo +beta-$TARGET test --release with the same features, cargo +$MSRV-$TARGET test --lib with fail‑on‑warnings num‑traits std, cargo +$MSRV-$TARGET test --release --lib with the same features, and cargo +nightly-$TARGET test with fail‑on‑warnings nightly‑float. Add an i686-gnulinux-tidy job that also caches cargo/, runs only on ci, installs the beta toolchain, adds rustfmt and clippy components, checks formatting with cargo +beta-$TARGET fmt -- --check, and runs cargo +beta-$TARGET clippy --all-targets with each of the fail‑on‑warnings feature sets (arbitrary borsh num‑traits serde std, arbitrary, num‑traits, borsh, serde, serde‑str, std, and the bare flag). Add analogous x86_64-gnulinux, x86_64-gnulinux-tidy, and x86_64-gnulinux-tarpaulin jobs using the amd64/rust:1 image and TARGET x86_64, where the tarpaulin job installs cargo‑tarpaulin version 0.32.0 and runs cargo +beta-$TARGET tarpaulin with a required coverage threshold $REQ_COVERAGE of 70. Finally, add a pages job that runs only on the www pipeline, caches cargo/, installs the nightly toolchain, clears and recreates a public directory, copies etc/index.html into it, generates documentation with cargo +nightly-$TARGET doc using the arbitrary borsh num‑traits serde std features and -Z rustdoc‑map, moves the generated docs to public/dev, and publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact.
